chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D-1310 of 2026 (Farooq Masih versus Province of Sindh & others) Date Order with signature of Judge Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi 1. For order on office objection Nos.1 and 2 2. For hearing of main case Date of hearing and order: 20.4.2026 Mr. Shahid Hussain advocate for the petitioner Mr. Asad Ahmed advocate for the respondent / KMC --------------------- ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. Petitioner Farooq Masih has filed this Constitution Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 seeking following relief:- a. To declare that the petitioner is legally entitled for recovery of retirement benefits Gratuity etc. from the respondents and the respondents are bound to act under the framework provided in constitution of Pakistan and to obey the law being the statutory body incorporated under the provincial government. b. To direct the respondents to release retirements benefits Gratuity etc. forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents being statutory body is bound to act in accordance with law and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the petitioner and respondent is bound under principle policy to do so. It appears that the petitioner was appointed on 10.10.1982 in BPS-02 in Sanitation Department DMC Korangi as Sanitary Worker and was retired from service on 03.6.2023, and since then his pensionary benefits have not yet been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that the petitioner is drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly; they received the pension file of the petitioner and the KMC has processed the pension case of the petitioner for amounting to Rs.2,415,171/-, which needs to be disbursed in the account of the petitioner within three weeks’ time.

It appears that the petitioner was appointed on 10.10.1982 in BPS-02 in Sanitation Department DMC Korangi as Sanitary Worker and was retired from service on 03.6.2023, and since then his pensionary benefits have not yet been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that the petitioner is drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly; they received the pension file of the petitioner and the KMC has processed the pension case of the petitioner for amounting to Rs.2,415,171/-, which needs to be disbursed in the account of the petitioner within three weeks’ time. The request seems to be reasonable and acceded to. When confronted the above position of the case, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of this petition in terms of the statement so made by the counsel for the KMC. Petition stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Zahid/*
